wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the WRD Photography portfolio project overview. Explain the project's purpose as a professional photography showcase website, its key features including interactive photo galleries, animated navigation, and real-time content management. Document the target audience (photography enthusiasts, art collectors, potential clients) and the artistic vision behind the Cambodia-focused street and rural photography collection. Include the technology stack summary covering Next.js 16, React 19, Sanity CMS, GSAP animations, and modern web development practices. Provide context about the project's role in digital portfolio presentation and online photography marketing. Use terminology consistent with the codebase and include practical examples of the visual impact and user experience offered by this portfolio solution.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive frontend architecture documentation for the Next.js App Router-based photography portfolio. Detail the component hierarchy starting from RootLayout through page components to reusable UI elements. Explain the integration of Next.js 16 App Router with server components and client components. Document the styling architecture using CSS custom properties, Tailwind CSS, and styled-components. Cover the font loading system with Google Fonts integration and font optimization. Explain the global state management approach and component communication patterns. Include responsive design implementation and mobile-first strategy. Document the asset management system including static files and image optimization. Address the build configuration and bundling strategy for optimal performance.

wiki_catalogs.name: Sanity CMS Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Sanity CMS studio interface and configuration. Explain the studio setup process, plugin configuration including structure tool and vision tool. Document the studio navigation patterns, content creation workflows, and real-time preview functionality. Detail the environment variable configuration for projectId, dataset, and API versioning. Explain the studio structure customization and how it organizes content sections. Include practical examples of content authoring, editing, and publishing processes. Document the relationship between studio configuration and frontend content consumption. Address studio permissions, collaboration features, and content scheduling capabilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the component architecture of the Next.js frontend application. Detail the component hierarchy starting from the root layout components through specialized page components. Document each major component including Nav.js for animated navigation, Cursor.js for custom cursor effects, FeaturedPage.js for photo slideshow, GalleryPage.js for multi-layout gallery display, AboutPage.js for artist biography presentation, and Lightbox.js for photo viewer. Explain component composition patterns, prop interfaces, state management integration, and lifecycle methods. Include component communication strategies, event handling patterns, and reusability considerations. Document component-specific styling approaches, animation integration, and accessibility implementations. Provide practical examples of component usage, customization options, and integration with the broader application architecture.

wiki_catalogs.name: Featured Photo Slideshow
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the featured photo slideshow system. Detail the navigation logic including mouse wheel and keyboard controls (arrow keys), animation sequences using GSAP timelines, and text reveal effects with staggered animations. Explain the counter display system showing current position and total count, dot indicators for photo navigation, and hint messages for user guidance. Document the image transition animations including scaling, rotation, and positioning effects during photo changes. Cover the responsive typography system with dynamic font sizing and line wrapping for titles. Include implementation details for state management, event handling, and performance optimizations. Provide examples of customization options for animation timing, easing functions, and visual effects.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component APIs
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive component API documentation for all React components in the WRD Photography portfolio. Document the Nav component's props (activePage, onNavigate), state management, and event handlers including theme switching functionality. Detail the Cursor component's mouse tracking API, animation parameters, and customization options. Document the FeaturedPage component's photo navigation API, animation controls, counter system, and text reveal functionality. Cover the GalleryPage component's layout system, photo filtering, and grid rendering APIs. Document the AboutPage component's content rendering and image collage API. Include the Lightbox component's modal display, photo navigation, and interaction handlers. Provide TypeScript interface definitions, prop validation requirements, and usage examples for each component. Document component lifecycle methods, ref handling, and integration patterns with GSAP animations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Image Loading Strategies
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for image loading strategies in the WRD Photography portfolio. Document the implementation of lazy loading for photo galleries, progressive loading techniques for featured slideshows, and CDN optimization through Sanity's image processing. Explain the image URL generation system, responsive image variants, and automatic format selection. Cover performance optimization techniques including image compression, quality settings, and aspect ratio handling. Document the integration between Sanity's image processing capabilities and Next.js image optimization. Include practical examples of implementing lazy loading in gallery components, optimizing image delivery for different screen sizes, and measuring image performance impact. Address memory management for large photo collections and browser caching strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Navigation Component
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Navigation component implementation. Document the animated navigation system including GSAP-powered entrance/exit animations, mouse proximity detection logic, and auto-hide functionality. Explain the component's props interface including activePage and onNavigate callback, state management for theme switching, and localStorage persistence. Detail the navigation links structure, hover effects, and visual indicators for active pages. Include implementation of the automatic hide/show behavior triggered by mouse movement, the theme toggle button with SVG icons, and responsive design considerations. Provide examples of component usage, customization options for styling and behavior, and integration patterns with the main application routing system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for the WRD Photography portfolio. Include detailed prerequisites covering Node.js version requirements, npm/yarn/pnpm/bun package managers, and development environment setup. Provide step-by-step installation instructions starting from repository cloning, dependency installation using package managers, and initial project setup. Document the development server startup process with multiple package manager options and local development server access. Explain environment variable configuration for both frontend and Sanity CMS integration. Include verification steps to ensure proper installation and initial setup validation. Address common setup issues and provide troubleshooting guidance for development environment problems. Make the content accessible to developers of all experience levels while ensuring they can quickly get the project running locally.

wiki_catalogs.name: CMS Integration Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed CMS integration architecture documentation for the Sanity.io headless CMS implementation. Explain the content modeling approach and document schemas for photos, about pages, and site settings. Document the GROQ query system and data fetching patterns used throughout the application. Detail the Sanity client configuration and authentication setup. Explain the real-time collaboration features and live preview functionality. Cover the content studio customization and admin interface configuration. Document the image processing pipeline and CDN integration. Include the deployment strategy for content changes and preview environments. Address content validation, field constraints, and data integrity measures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Content Schemas and Data Models
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for all content schemas and data models used in the portfolio. Document the Photo schema with comprehensive field definitions including title, image asset, location, series classification, featured status, date, writeup text, and ordering. Explain the About Page schema covering hero image configuration and collage image arrangements. Detail the Site Settings schema for global configuration options. Document field validation rules, required/optional field specifications, and data type constraints. Explain the schema relationship patterns and how content types interact. Include practical examples of content creation and field usage. Document schema evolution practices and backward compatibility considerations. Address content modeling best practices and field naming conventions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Styling System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the styling system implementation in the Next.js application. Document the CSS custom properties architecture used for theming, including dark/light mode switching and color scheme management. Explain the integration with Tailwind CSS 4 for utility-first styling and responsive design patterns. Detail the global styling approach in globals.css, including font loading, base styles, and theme variables. Document the image optimization system and CDN integration for performance. Explain responsive design implementation across different screen sizes, mobile-first approach, and breakpoint strategies. Include CSS-in-JS considerations, styled-components usage, and component-specific styling patterns. Address performance optimization techniques including critical CSS, CSS minification, and asset optimization. Provide examples of theme customization, responsive breakpoints, and styling best practices.

wiki_catalogs.name: Photo Gallery System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the multi-layout gallery system. Explain the implementation of different gallery layouts including horizontal street strip, masonry rural/landscape grids, and portrait grid systems. Document the responsive design patterns that adapt layouts based on screen size and photo orientation. Cover the lightbox viewer functionality with modal display, navigation controls, and image optimization. Detail the photo filtering and sorting mechanisms, lazy loading implementations, and performance optimizations for large photo collections. Include examples of customizing grid layouts, adding new gallery types, and implementing additional photo viewing modes. Address accessibility considerations for keyboard navigation and screen reader compatibility.

wiki_catalogs.name: Sanity CMS API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed Sanity CMS API documentation covering the client configuration, query methods, and content management interfaces. Document the Sanity client initialization, authentication setup, and configuration options. Detail the GROQ query API including query construction, parameter binding, and result processing. Cover the image processing API with URL generation, transformation options, and optimization parameters. Document the live preview API for real-time content updates and collaborative editing features. Include error handling patterns, retry mechanisms, and performance optimization techniques. Provide practical examples of common query patterns for photos, about pages, and site settings. Document the content schema integration and validation processes. Include authentication methods, access control, and security considerations for the CMS API.

wiki_catalogs.name: Build Optimization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive build optimization documentation for the Next.js application. Detail Next.js specific optimizations including static generation configuration, code splitting strategies, and bundle analysis techniques. Document PostCSS optimization including Tailwind CSS purging, CSS minification, and critical CSS extraction. Explain webpack configuration for asset optimization, dependency optimization, and tree shaking implementation. Cover build-time image optimization, asset compression, and deployment optimization strategies. Document environment-specific optimizations and production build configurations. Include practical examples of build performance monitoring, bundle size analysis, and optimization techniques. Address incremental static regeneration and ISR optimization for dynamic content. Provide guidance on build caching, parallel builds, and CI/CD pipeline optimization.

wiki_catalogs.name: Custom Cursor Component
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Custom Cursor component implementation. Document the cursor effect system including mouse tracking, element detection, scaling animations, and interactive behaviors. Explain the component's positioning logic, collision detection with interactive elements, and GSAP-powered animations for hover states. Detail the cursor variants (default, hover, click), performance optimizations for smooth tracking, and integration with page navigation. Include implementation of the cursor trail effects, element interaction detection, and responsive behavior across different screen sizes. Provide examples of cursor customization, performance considerations for smooth animations, and integration with other interactive elements throughout the application.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the WRD Photography portfolio system. Describe the high-level design combining Next.js App Router frontend with Sanity CMS backend, explaining the content-driven architecture pattern. Document the component hierarchy from root layout through navigation to individual page components. Explain the data flow from Sanity Studio content creation through GROQ queries to React components. Detail the animation architecture using GSAP for micro-interactions and page transitions. Include system boundaries between frontend presentation layer and backend content management. Document the integration patterns between Next.js static generation, server components, and client-side interactivity. Address the responsive design architecture and mobile-first approach. Explain the theme system architecture using CSS custom properties and styled-components. Include infrastructure considerations for deployment and performance optimization.

wiki_catalogs.name: Animation System Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive animation system architecture documentation focusing on GSAP integration and animation patterns. Detail the animation controller architecture and how GSAP orchestrates micro-interactions throughout the application. Explain the page transition system and navigation animations. Document the custom cursor animation implementation and interactive hover effects. Cover the animation performance optimization strategies and browser compatibility considerations. Explain the relationship between animation timing, user interaction, and visual feedback systems. Include the animation skill documentation and GSAP plugin architecture. Address animation debugging tools and performance monitoring approaches.

wiki_catalogs.name: GROQ Queries and Data Access
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for GROQ query language implementation and data access patterns. Explain the GROQ syntax basics and query construction techniques used throughout the application. Document each query function in queries.js including featured photos retrieval, gallery content fetching, about page data, and site settings. Detail the Sanity client configuration and initialization process. Explain query optimization strategies, caching mechanisms, and performance considerations. Include practical examples of common query patterns for different content types. Document error handling strategies and query debugging techniques. Address real-time data updates through the live preview system and how queries integrate with the frontend components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Animation System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the GSAP-based animation system implementation. Detail the GSAP integration setup, timeline creation patterns, and animation sequencing for smooth transitions. Document the animation architecture including page transitions, photo slideshow animations, navigation hover effects, and cursor interactions. Explain the animation controller patterns, state-driven animations, and performance optimization techniques. Cover micro-interaction implementations including mouse proximity detection, hover states, and entrance/exit animations. Document animation timing functions, easing curves, and duration strategies. Include accessibility considerations for motion preferences, reduced motion support, and animation performance impact. Provide examples of custom animation creation, animation chaining, and integration with React state management. Address debugging techniques, performance monitoring, and browser compatibility approaches.

wiki_catalogs.name: About Page Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the about page implementation featuring artist biography display, philosophy sections, and responsive collage layouts. Document the content structure including hero image presentation, text formatting with custom typography, and image collage arrangements. Explain the responsive design patterns that ensure optimal display across different screen sizes and device orientations. Cover the content management integration with Sanity CMS for easy updates to artist information and philosophy statements. Include implementation details for image optimization, loading strategies, and accessibility features. Provide examples of customizing the layout, adding new content sections, and modifying the visual presentation of biographical information.

wiki_catalogs.name: Utility APIs
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive utility API documentation for helper functions and shared utilities used throughout the application. Document the GROQ query utility functions including content fetching helpers, pagination methods, and filter operations. Detail the image processing utilities covering URL generation, transformation parameters, CDN integration, and optimization settings. Cover CSS utility functions for theme management, responsive breakpoints, and animation timing. Document localStorage utilities for theme persistence and user preferences. Include font loading utilities, asset optimization helpers, and performance monitoring functions. Provide usage examples, parameter specifications, and integration patterns for each utility function. Document error handling, fallback mechanisms, and browser compatibility considerations for utility APIs.

wiki_catalogs.name: Runtime Performance
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed runtime performance documentation focusing on component-level optimizations and user experience enhancements. Document animation performance tuning using GSAP, including frame rate optimization, GPU acceleration, and animation batching. Explain component memoization strategies, React performance hooks, and state management optimization. Cover memory management techniques for large photo collections, efficient event handling, and DOM manipulation optimization. Document performance monitoring during user interactions, including input delay optimization and smooth scrolling implementation. Address throttling strategies for animations, debouncing techniques for resize events, and efficient rendering patterns. Include practical examples of performance profiling, identifying bottlenecks, and implementing performance improvements. Cover mobile-specific optimizations and touch interaction performance considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Featured Photo Slideshow
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Featured Photo Slideshow component implementation. Document the full-screen photo presentation system including navigation controls (mouse wheel, keyboard), text reveal animations, photo counter display, and dot indicator system. Explain the GSAP-powered transition animations, swipe gesture support, and responsive image handling. Detail the component's state management for current photo tracking, navigation logic for previous/next functionality, and accessibility features. Include implementation of the photo loading system, lazy loading strategies, and performance optimizations. Provide examples of slideshow customization, animation timing adjustments, and integration with the main navigation system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Application
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Next.js frontend application architecture. Explain the component-based structure with detailed coverage of each major component including navigation system, custom cursor effects, featured photo slideshow, gallery layouts, about page, and lightbox viewer. Document the styling strategy using CSS custom properties, Tailwind CSS integration, and responsive design patterns. Detail the animation system implementation with GSAP integration for smooth transitions and micro-interactions. Explain the state management patterns, component communication, and lifecycle management. Include component composition strategies, prop interfaces, and reusability patterns. Provide practical examples of component usage and customization options.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Flow Patterns
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed data flow architecture documentation explaining the content-driven development pattern. Document the data pathway from Sanity Studio content creation through GROQ queries to React component rendering. Cover the caching mechanisms and performance optimization techniques for content delivery. Explain the relationship between content schemas and component prop structures. Document error handling and fallback strategies for content loading failures. Include the data validation patterns and content transformation processes.

Explain the image URL generation process, transformation options, and optimization settings. Document the image asset handling workflow from upload to display. Include practical examples of image transformations and cropping techniques. Document the relationship between Sanity image assets and frontend rendering. Address image loading performance, lazy loading implementation, and fallback strategies. Explain the integration with Next.js image optimization and how images are cached and served efficiently.

wiki_catalogs.name: State Management

Include practical examples of implementing performance improvements, measuring impact, and maintaining optimal scores. Address server-side rendering optimization, hydration performance, and initial page load optimization. Provide guidance on performance testing tools, automated monitoring, and performance budget enforcement.

wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Photo Gallery System component implementation. wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive performance optimization documentation for the WRD Photography portfolio. wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# WRD Photography Portfolio - Project Overview

## 1. Project Introduction

### Purpose Statement
WRD Photography is a modern, immersive photography portfolio website showcasing street and rural photography from Cambodia. The project serves as both an artistic showcase and a content-managed digital platform for a professional photographer's work.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Content Management**: Provide a robust CMS backend for managing photography collections through Sanity.io
- **Visual Excellence**: Deliver stunning, high-performance photo galleries with smooth animations
- **User Experience**: Create an engaging browsing experience with interactive elements and seamless navigation
- **Performance**: Optimize loading times and visual quality for photography-focused content

### Target Audience
- Photography enthusiasts and art collectors
- Potential clients seeking portfolio examples
- Art galleries and cultural institutions
- General web visitors interested in Cambodian photography

## 2. Technical Architecture

### Component Breakdown
The project follows a modern Next.js architecture with clear separation between frontend presentation and content management:

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Next.js App Router] --> B[Main Layout]
    A --> C[Dynamic Pages]
    B --> D[Navigation Component]
    B --> E[Cursor Component]
    C --> F[Featured Page]
    C --> G[Gallery Page]
    C --> H[About Page]
    F --> I[Sanity Client]
    G --> I
    H --> I
    I --> J[Sanity Studio]
    J --> K[Content Schema]
    K --> L[Photo Documents]
    K --> M[About Page Content]
```

### Design Patterns
- **Component-Based Architecture**: Modular React components with clear responsibilities
- **Content-Driven Development**: Data fetching from Sanity CMS with GROQ queries
- **Animation-First Approach**: GSAP-powered transitions and micro-interactions
- **Progressive Enhancement**: Graceful degradation for different interaction methods

### System Relationships
The application maintains a clean data flow from content management to presentation:

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant User
    participant Next.js App
    participant Sanity Client
    participant GROQ Queries
    participant Sanity Studio
    
    User->>Next.js App: Browse Portfolio
    Next.js App->>Sanity Client: Fetch Content
    Sanity Client->>GROQ Queries: Execute Queries
    GROQ Queries->>Sanity Studio: Retrieve Data
    Sanity Studio-->>GROQ Queries: Return JSON
    GROQ Queries-->>Sanity Client: Processed Data
    Sanity Client-->>Next.js App: Photo Collections
    Next.js App-->>User: Rendered Gallery
```

## 3. Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points
- **Application Root**: `app/layout.js` - Global layout with font loading and theme management
- **Home Page**: `app/page.js` - Main landing page with intro animation and navigation
- **Studio Route**: `app/studio/[[...tool]]/page.jsx` - Sanity Studio interface

### Core Modules
- **Content Management**: `sanity/lib/client.js` - Sanity client configuration
- **Data Queries**: `sanity/lib/queries.js` - GROQ queries for different content types
- **Image Processing**: `sanity/lib/image.js` - Image URL generation and optimization
- **Live Preview**: `sanity/lib/live.js` - Real-time content updates

### Configuration Approach
- **Framework Config**: `next.config.mjs` - Next.js build configuration
- **ESLint Config**: `eslint.config.mjs` - Code quality and formatting rules
- **PostCSS Config**: `postcss.config.mjs` - CSS processing pipeline
- **Tailwind Setup**: `app/globals.css` - Utility-first styling foundation

### External Dependencies
- **React 19**: Latest React features with server components support
- **Next.js 16**: App router with enhanced performance
- **Sanity 5**: Headless CMS with real-time collaboration
- **GSAP 3.15**: High-performance animation library
- **Styled Components**: CSS-in-JS for theming and animations

### Integration Points
- **Sanity Studio**: `/studio` route for content management
- **Image CDN**: Optimized image delivery through Sanity
- **Font Loading**: Google Fonts integration via Next.js font optimization
- **Theme System**: CSS custom properties for dark/light mode switching

### Component Relationship Diagram

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[Root Layout] --> B[Navigation]
    A --> C[Cursor]
    A --> D[Page Container]
    
    D --> E[Featured Page]
    D --> F[Gallery Page]
    D --> G[About Page]
    
    E --> H[Photo Collection]
    F --> H
    G --> I[About Content]
    
    H --> J[Sanity Client]
    I --> J
    J --> K[GROQ Queries]
    
    style A fill:#f9f9f9
    style D fill:#e1f5fe
    style J fill:#f3e5f5
```

## 4. Key Features

### Functionality Overview
- **Interactive Photo Slideshow**: Full-screen featured photo presentation with keyboard/mouse navigation
- **Smooth Animations**: GSAP-powered transitions between photos and page states
- **Real-time Content Updates**: Live preview capabilities in the CMS
- **Responsive Design**: Optimized viewing experience across all devices
- **Accessibility**: Proper semantic markup and keyboard navigation support

### Implementation Highlights
- **Intro Animation**: Custom SVG-based loading screen with animated text reveal
- **Photo Navigation**: Wheel/keyboard controls with momentum-based transitions
- **Cursor Effects**: Custom mouse cursor with ring and dot elements
- **Theme System**: CSS custom properties enabling dark/light mode switching
- **Image Optimization**: Automatic resizing and quality optimization

### Feature Architecture Diagram

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Loading
    Loading --> IntroAnimation: Fonts Ready
    IntroAnimation --> HomePage: Animation Complete
    HomePage --> FeaturedView: Navigate
    HomePage --> GalleryView: Navigate
    HomePage --> AboutView: Navigate
    
    FeaturedView --> PhotoTransition: Scroll/Key Input
    PhotoTransition --> FeaturedView: Transition Complete
    
    GalleryView --> PhotoDetail: Click Photo
    PhotoDetail --> GalleryView: Close Lightbox
    
    AboutView --> HomePage: Back Navigation
    
    HomePage --> Loading: Page Refresh
    FeaturedView --> Loading: Page Refresh
    GalleryView --> Loading: Page Refresh
    AboutView --> Loading: Page Refresh
```

### Performance Considerations
- **Code Splitting**: Dynamic imports for page components
- **Image Optimization**: Automatic format conversion and compression
- **Font Loading**: Critical font subset loading for faster initial render
- **Animation Optimization**: GPU-accelerated transforms and opacity changes
- **Memory Management**: Proper cleanup of event listeners and timeouts

The project demonstrates a sophisticated approach to combining modern web technologies with artistic content presentation, creating a professional portfolio solution that prioritizes both visual impact and technical excellence.

</blog>
